meta.title: World Cup 2026 Simulator
meta.description: Monte Carlo with ELO + Poisson. 100,000 simulations of the FIFA World Cup 2026.

hero.subtitle: ELO-based model with Poisson goals. 48 teams, 104 matches, billions of possible outcomes — you choose how many we simulate.

stages.title: Stage probabilities
stages.subtitle: Probability of each team reaching each round.

bracket.title: Most likely bracket
bracket.subtitle: Most frequent path to the cup.
bracket.hover_hint: Hover any match for its probability.

methodology.title: Methodology
methodology.lead: How the simulator works, no secrets. Same approach as the original video (ELO + Poisson + Monte Carlo), built from scratch in pure TypeScript.
methodology.elo_body: We use ratings from eloratings.net, NOT the FIFA ranking. Two different systems: ELO is a recursive (chess-style) system that updates after every international match since 1872 and tracks recent form much more faithfully. Belgium was FIFA #1 for years without major titles; ELO never put them there.
methodology.elo_explain: dr = ELO gap + 100 if playing at home (USA, Mexico, Canada during group stage).
methodology.poisson_body: For each match, each team's goals are drawn from an independent Poisson distribution. The parameter λ (expected goals) depends on the ELO gap:
methodology.poisson_explain: BASE = 1.30 (avg goals per team in recent World Cups). ALPHA = 0.18 (sensitivity to ELO gap, calibrated against historical scoring). If your team has 200 ELO points over the opponent, you expect ~0.36 more goals. λ is clamped to [0.15, 6.0] to keep the distribution sane.
methodology.groups_body: 12 groups × 6 matches × 4 teams = 72 matches. Points: 3/1/0. Tiebreakers in order: points → goal difference → goals scored → random. Top 2 from each group + 8 best 3rd-placed teams advance (ranked by the same criteria across groups).
methodology.knockout_body: R32 → R16 → QF → SF → 3rd-place + Final. If regulation ends tied, a coin flip decides (simplified penalty-shootout model: both teams convert at the same rate, so it's 50/50). Penalty goals are NOT added to stats.
methodology.knockout_note: 3rd-place placement: the 495 combinations from FIFA Annex C are approximated — we rank all 12 third-placed teams, take the 8 best, and place them in the bracket's 8 'best 3rd' slots respecting FIFA's group constraints.
methodology.mc_body: We run N full tournaments (1k / 10k / 50k / 100k, your choice). Each tournament is independent. Final stats are relative frequencies: 'Argentina won 14,900 of 100,000 tournaments → 14.90% probability'.
methodology.perf_body: Pure-TypeScript engine with xoshiro128** PRNG (faster and better-distributed than Math.random) and Knuth's Poisson sampler. 100k sims run in ~3s in Node and ~5-8s in browser via Web Worker.
methodology.verify_item_2: Avg goals per match = 2.6 (consistent with World Cup history: 2.5-2.7)
methodology.verify_item_3: Top-5 contenders match bookmaker consensus (Spain, Argentina, France, Brazil, Portugal)
methodology.limits_body: Individual scorers. Injuries. Manager changes. Form of the day. The draw (we take it as given). And deliberately: we DON'T tune the model to 'fix' weird outcomes — if the model says something absurd, that's information, not a bug.
